title: premature-GC use-after-free: managed object passed to native WMI call is not kept alive
category: premature-GC use-after-free across the managed ↔ native (COM) boundary
crash_site: Intermittent access violation / heap corruption inside an unmanaged COM interface call (IWbemClassObject) during object comparison and method-signature operations, only under GC pressure. The managed wrapper already pins its own instance across the call, so it looks correct.

ground_truth.decoy_fn: the existing GC.KeepAlive(this) at the end of each method (the receiver IS kept alive — looks complete). PutMethod_ has the identical bug. The IWbemClassObjectFreeThreaded finalizer is the plausible-but-wrong place to look.
ground_truth.fix_summary: CompareTo_/PutMethod_ pass the IntPtr handle of their *argument* wrappers (pCompareTo; pInSignature/pOutSignature) into native WMI calls but only call GC.KeepAlive(this) — the argument wrappers could be collected mid-call (their finalizer freeing the COM object the native code still uses). Fix: add GC.KeepAlive(pCompareTo) in CompareTo_ and GC.KeepAlive(pInSignature)/GC.KeepAlive(pOutSignature) in PutMethod_ after the native call.

admission.note: Authored from PR #124796 body+diff (PR notes the issue was surfaced by an LLM, 'Opus'). crash != cause: AV fires in native WMI; cause is a missing GC.KeepAlive on the *argument* object in CompareTo_/PutMethod_ — decoy is the present GC.KeepAlive(this). Defensive fix (author couldn't fully prove the race) -> grade ownership/lifetime correctness. Contamination LOW (obscure interop file). SINGLE FILE: if baseline FULL-solves by grepping WMIInterop.cs, down-weight. Cross-boundary: C# managed lifetime <-> native COM. Run baseline first.

provenance: https://github.com/dotnet/runtime/pull/124796 — Add missing GC.KeepAlives to WMIInterop; fix commit adc191279b42, base 0f125741186d. Single-file. The C#↔native twin of node-56840 (premature-GC UAF). AI-surfaced (Opus) — viral hook, but obscure file keeps contamination low.
